notes.sg-tissue-engineering-regenerative-medic: Regenerative medicine is a broad field that heavily relies on stem cells, making it a good candidate to merge into 'stem cell'.
notes.sg-tissue-engineering-cell-biology: Cell biology is a foundational science for tissue engineering and stem cell research. Merging into 'stem cell' captures the applied aspect.
notes.sg-tissue-engineering-stem-cell: This is a core concept and can serve as a good anchor for related topics.
notes.sg-tissue-engineering-cell-culture: Cell culture is a technique often used in stem cell research and tissue engineering. Merging into 'stem cell' is appropriate.
notes.sg-tissue-engineering-3d-bioprinting: 3D bioprinting is a method that utilizes cells and biomaterials, often stem cells, for tissue engineering. Merging into the more general '3D printing in biomedical research' is suitable.
notes.sg-tissue-engineering-self-healing-hydro: Self-healing hydrogels are a material science aspect of tissue engineering, distinct from the biological focus of other topics.
notes.sg-tissue-engineering-extracellular-matr: Extracellular matrix is a key component in tissue engineering.
notes.sg-tissue-engineering-mesenchymal-stem-c: Mesenchymal stem cells are a specific type of stem cell relevant to tissue engineering.
notes.sg-tissue-engineering-natural-polymers: Natural polymers are important biomaterials used in tissue engineering.
